title_en: Salah in Al-Mabsut: Prayer Law and Comparative Analysis
title_ar: الصلاة في المبسوط: أحكام الصلاة وتحليلها المقارن
content_en:
As-Sarakhsi's prayer chapters in Al-Mabsut combine comprehensive coverage of Hanafi prayer law with systematic comparison with other schools, making it an invaluable resource for anyone seeking to understand both Hanafi prayer law and the comparative jurisprudence of salah. He engages particularly with ash-Shafi'i's positions, presenting the intellectual debate between the two schools with scholarly rigor.

A prominent comparative question is the recitation of al-Fatihah. The Hanafi school holds that al-Fatihah is wajib (obligatory) rather than a rukn (pillar) — its omission requires sujud as-sahw if forgetful, and invalidates the prayer if intentional, but the theoretical distinction from the Shafi'i position has practical implications. As-Sarakhsi engages the Shafi'i argument (the hadith 'There is no prayer without al-Fatihah') and the Hanafi counter-argument (other Quranic verses refer to 'reciting what is easy of Quran' without specifying al-Fatihah, and some hadiths mention prayer being valid without specifying al-Fatihah). This evidential engagement is as-Sarakhsi at his best.

The Hanafi school's distinctive position on the follower's recitation behind an imam receives detailed treatment. In the Hanafi school, the follower does not recite al-Fatihah or any other Quranic text when praying behind an imam — the imam's recitation suffices. As-Sarakhsi defends this position with the hadith: 'Whoever has an imam, the imam's recitation is his recitation' (Ibn Majah, reported with various chains), and the Quranic verse: 'When the Quran is recited, listen to it and be silent, so that you may receive mercy' (7:204) — which the Hanafi school applies to prayer specifically.

As-Sarakhsi discusses the prayer of the sick person in detail, applying the Islamic principle of graduated ease. The sick person who cannot stand prays sitting; if they cannot sit, they pray on their side; if they cannot do that, they gesture with their eyes. The Prophet's instruction to 'Imran ibn Husayn — 'Pray standing; if you cannot, then sitting; if you cannot, then on your side' — is the primary evidence. As-Sarakhsi addresses the question of what threshold of illness justifies sitting prayer — he defines it as illness causing significant pain or hardship in standing, not mere discomfort.

The chapter on Friday prayer (Jumu'ah) illustrates the Hanafi school's emphasis on communal worship. The Hanafi school holds that Jumu'ah requires a minimum of three qualified male attendees (besides the imam) — a notably low minimum compared to the Shafi'i requirement of forty. As-Sarakhsi defends this position by arguing that the Quranic command to 'hasten to the remembrance of Allah' (62:9) does not specify a minimum number, and the prophetic practice in Medina set no explicit numerical threshold.

The prayer of the traveler — its shortening (qasr) and combining (jama') — is treated with attention to the specific distances and conditions that trigger each concession. As-Sarakhsi notes that the Prophet consistently shortened his prayers during travel and that scholars who said he prayed full prayers during travel have confused the voluntary prayers he performed separately with the obligatory prayers, which he always shortened.
title_id: Salat dalam Al-Mabsut: Hukum Salat dan Analisis Komparatif
content_id:
Bab-bab salat dalam Al-Mabsut as-Sarakhsi menggabungkan cakupan komprehensif tentang hukum salat Hanafi dengan perbandingan sistematis dengan mazhab-mazhab lain, menjadikannya sumber yang sangat berharga bagi siapa pun yang ingin memahami hukum salat Hanafi maupun perdebatan perbandingan mazhab dalam bidang ini.

As-Sarakhsi membahas azan dan iqamah dengan rinci, termasuk perbedaan antara Hanafi dan mazhab lain tentang lafal-lafal tertentu. Beliau kemudian beralih ke syarat-syarat sah salat: wudhu, menutupi aurat, menghadap kiblat, dan waktu salat. Penentuan waktu-waktu salat mendapat perhatian khusus, dengan as-Sarakhsi menjelaskan pandangan Hanafi bahwa waktu Isya berakhir pada sepertiga malam pertama (bukan pertengahan malam) dan berbagai perbedaan lainnya dari mazhab Syafi'i.

Rukun-rukun salat dibahas satu per satu: niat, takbir al-ihram, membaca Al-Fatihah di belakang imam (Hanafi berpendapat tidak perlu), ruku', sujud, duduk terakhir, dan salam. As-Sarakhsi sangat teliti dalam menjelaskan posisi Hanafi tentang bacaan Al-Fatihah di belakang imam — yang berbeda dari mazhab lain — dengan menyajikan dalil-dalilnya dan merespons argumen mazhab Syafi'i.

Salat berjamaah, salat Jumat, salat sunnah rawatib, salat witir, dan salat-salat dalam kondisi khusus (salat dalam perjalanan, salat Ied, salat gerhana, salat istisqa') semuanya mendapat pembahasan yang terperinci dan komprehensif. Seluruh bagian ini mencerminkan kedalaman dan keluasan fiqh salat dalam tradisi Hanafi sebagaimana dikembangkan oleh as-Sarakhsi.
content_ar:
أبواب الصلاة في المبسوط من أوفى ما كُتب في الفقه الحنفي الكلاسيكي، وفيها يبلغ منهج السرخسي المقارن والتأصيلي ذروته. يبدأ بشروط صحة الصلاة: الطهارة، وستر العورة، واستقبال القبلة، ودخول الوقت، والنية. ويُسهب في كل شرط موضحاً دليله وما يتفرع عنه من مسائل.

والأذان والإقامة يأخذان حيزاً واسعاً، مع بيان الألفاظ المأثورة وخلاف العلماء في صيغة الأذان بين التثويب وتركه وعدد التكبيرات. ثم ينتقل إلى أركان الصلاة وواجباتها وسننها وما يُفسدها وما يوجب سجود السهو.

ومن أبرز مسائل الصلاة عند الحنفية التي يعالجها السرخسي بعمق: الجهر بالبسملة وعدمه، وقراءة الفاتحة خلف الإمام، إذ يُقرر المذهب الحنفي عدم وجوب قراءة المأموم خلف إمامه، مستنداً إلى قوله تعالى: {وَإِذَا قُرِئَ الْقُرْآنُ فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ وَأَنصِتُوا}، وإلى جملة من الأحاديث. ويستوفي السرخسي حجج الشافعية ثم يردّ عليها بالتوجيه والترجيح.

وصلاة الجماعة وشروط الإمامة وترتيب الأولوية فيها مباحث يُفيض فيها السرخسي. كما يتناول صلاة المسافر وأحكام القصر والجمع، والصلوات ذوات الأسباب كصلاة الجنازة والعيدين والكسوف والاستسقاء. وفي كل ذلك يُقابل موقف المذهب الحنفي بما يقوله المالكية والشافعية والحنابلة، مع التعليل والاستدلال.
